title: Nephelium glabrum
intro: Nephelium glabrum, also known as the rambai tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The rambai tree is valued for its edible fruit and leaves, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Nephelium glabrum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, the rambai tree is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Nephelium glabrum is classified in the family Sapindaceae. This classification places it within the order Sapindales. Further, it is classified as a species within the genus Nephelium. The Sapindaceae family comprises a range of trees and shrubs commonly known as the soapberry or soapnut family. Nephelium glabrum is native to Southeast Asia.
uses: Nephelium glabrum, also known as the rambai fruit, is native to Malaysia and Singapore. Research on its medicinal properties is ongoing, but some studies suggest that it has potential health benefits. The fruit is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. More studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. The rambai fruit may also have anti-inflammatory properties, which could contribute to its potential health benefits.
benefits: Nephelium glabrum, a lesser-known species of the rambutan family, has been found to possess several health benefits. Its fruit is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. constituents: Nephelium glabrum is a species of the Rambutan family, and like other plants in the same family, it is rich in various active constituents. These active constituents may include flavonoids, tannins, and other polyphenolic compounds. Flavonoids are known for their antioxidant properties and may contribute to the plant's potential health benefits. preparations: Nephelium glabrum is a plant species that is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia.
